content: eino里怎么处理中断与恢复
我来帮你了解 Eino 中如何处理中断与恢复。这涉及到 human-in-the-loop 的实现，让我先加载相关的技能文档。

1	Human-in-the-loop (HITL) enables agents to pause execution, request human input, and resume from where they stopped.
     2	
     3	## Core Concepts
     4	
     5	1. **Interrupt**: Agent pauses and sends info to the user (e.g., "approve this action?")
     6	2. **Checkpoint**: Framework saves execution state to a CheckPointStore
     7	3. **Resume**: User provides input, framework restores state and continues
     8	
     9	## Quick Start: Approval Pattern
    10	
    11	### 1. Create a tool that can interrupt
    12	
    13	```go
    14	import (
    15	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/adk"
    16	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/components/tool"
    17	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/components/tool/utils"
    18	    "github.com/cloudwego/eino/compose"
    19	)
    20	
    21	type bookInput struct {
    22	    Location      string `json:"location"`
    23	    PassengerName string `json:"passenger_name"`
    24	}
    25	
    26	bookTool, _ := utils.InferTool("BookTicket", "Book a ticket",
    27	    func(ctx context.Context, input *bookInput) (string, error) {
    28	        return "success", nil
    29	    })
    30	
    31	// Wrap with approval -- interrupts before execution
    32	approvalTool := &tool.InvokableApprovableTool{InvokableTool: bookTool}
    33	```
    34	
    35	### 2. Create agent and runner with CheckPointStore
    36	
    37	```go
    38	agent, _ := adk.NewChatModelAgent(ctx, &adk.ChatModelAgentConfig{
    39	    Name:        "TicketBooker",
    40	    Instruction: "Book tickets using the BookTicket tool.",
    41	    Model:       cm,
    42	    ToolsConfig: adk.ToolsConfig{
    43	        ToolsNodeConfig: compose.ToolsNodeConfig{
    44	            Tools: []tool.BaseTool{approvalTool},
    45	        },
    46	    },
    47	})
    48	
    49	runner := adk.NewRunner(ctx, adk.RunnerConfig{
    50	    Agent:           agent,
    51	    EnableStreaming:  true,
    52	    CheckPointStore: store.NewInMemoryStore(), // Use Redis in production
    53	})
    54	```
    55	
    56	### 3. Run and handle interrupt
    57	
    58	```go
    59	iter := runner.Query(ctx, "Book a ticket to Beijing for Martin", adk.WithCheckPointID("session-1"))
    60	
    61	var interruptID string
    62	for {
    63	    event, ok := iter.Next()
    64	    if !ok {
    65	        break
    66	    }
    67	    if event.Err != nil {
    68	        log.Fatal(event.Err)
    69	    }
    70	    if event.Action != nil && event.Action.Interrupted != nil {
    71	        // Save the interrupt ID for resuming later
    72	        interruptID = event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts[0].ID
    73	        fmt.Printf("Interrupt: %v\n", event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts[0].Info)
    74	        break
    75	    }
    76	    // Handle normal output...
    77	}
    78	```
    79	
    80	### 4. Resume with user decision
    81	
    82	```go
    83	// User approves
    84	apResult := &tool.ApprovalResult{Approved: true}
    85	
    86	iter, err := runner.ResumeWithParams(ctx, "session-1", &adk.ResumeParams{
    87	    Targets: map[string]any{
    88	        interruptID: apResult,
    89	    },
    90	})
    91	if err != nil {
    92	    log.Fatal(err)
    93	}
    94	
    95	// Continue processing events
    96	for {
    97	    event, ok := iter.Next()
    98	    if !ok {
    99	        break
   100	    }
   101	    // Handle events...
   102	}
   103	```
   104	
   105	## Interrupt APIs (ADK Layer)
   106	
   107	### Simple Interrupt
   108	
   109	```go
   110	// In a custom agent's Run method:
   111	return adk.Interrupt(ctx, "Please clarify your request.")
   112	```
   113	
   114	### Stateful Interrupt
   115	
   116	```go
   117	// Save internal state for later restoration
   118	state := &MyState{ProcessedItems: 42}
   119	return adk.StatefulInterrupt(ctx, "Need user feedback", state)
   120	```
   121	
   122	### Composite Interrupt (for multi-agent)
   123	
   124	```go
   125	// When a parent agent's sub-agent interrupts
   126	return adk.CompositeInterrupt(ctx, "Sub-agent needs attention", parentState, subInterruptSignals...)
   127	```
   128	
   129	## Interrupt APIs (Compose Layer -- for tools/graph nodes)
   130	
   131	```go
   132	import "github.com/cloudwego/eino/compose"
   133	
   134	// Simple interrupt from a tool
   135	return "", compose.Interrupt(ctx, "Waiting for approval")
   136	
   137	// Stateful interrupt
   138	return "", compose.StatefulInterrupt(ctx, "Need input", myState)
   139	```
   140	
   141	## InterruptInfo Structure
   142	
   143	When an interrupt occurs, the event contains structured info:
   144	
   145	```go
   146	if event.Action != nil && event.Action.Interrupted != nil {
   147	    for _, point := range event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts {
   148	        fmt.Printf("ID: %s\n", point.ID)           // Unique interrupt address
   149	        fmt.Printf("Info: %v\n", point.Info)         // User-facing info
   150	        fmt.Printf("Root cause: %v\n", point.IsRootCause)
   151	    }
   152	}
   153	```
   154	
   155	## Resume APIs
   156	
   157	### ResumeWithParams (recommended)
   158	
   159	```go
   160	iter, err := runner.ResumeWithParams(ctx, checkpointID, &adk.ResumeParams{
   161	    Targets: map[string]any{
   162	        interruptID1: userData1,
   163	        interruptID2: userData2,
   164	    },
   165	})
   166	```
   167	
   168	### Legacy Resume (with tool options)
   169	
   170	```go
   171	iter, err := runner.Resume(ctx, checkpointID,
   172	    adk.WithToolOptions([]tool.Option{WithNewInput("user response")}),
   173	)
   174	```
   175	
   176	## ResumableAgent Interface
   177	
   178	Agents that support interrupt/resume must implement:
   179	
   180	```go
   181	type ResumableAgent interface {
   182	    Agent
   183	    Resume(ctx context.Context, info *ResumeInfo, opts ...AgentRunOption) *AsyncIterator[*AgentEvent]
   184	}
   185	
   186	type ResumeInfo struct {
   187	    WasInterrupted bool   // Always true when Resume is called
   188	    InterruptState any    // State saved via StatefulInterrupt
   189	    IsResumeTarget bool   // Whether this agent is the explicit resume target
   190	    ResumeData     any    // User-provided data for this agent
   191	}
   192	```
   193	
   194	ChatModelAgent implements ResumableAgent by default.
   195	
   196	## CheckPointStore
   197	
   198	```go
   199	type CheckPointStore interface {
   200	    Set(ctx context.Context, key string, value []byte) error
   201	    Get(ctx context.Context, key string) ([]byte, bool, error)
   202	}
   203	```
   204	
   205	Built-in: `store.NewInMemoryStore()` (for development). Use Redis or similar for production.
   206	
   207	State serialization uses `encoding/gob`. Register custom types:
   208	
   209	```go
   210	func init() {
   211	    gob.RegisterName("mypackage.MyType", &MyType{})
   212	}
   213	```
   214	
   215	## Common HITL Patterns
   216	
   217	| Pattern | Description | Example |
   218	|---------|-------------|---------|
   219	| Approval | Pause before executing an action | Tool execution approval |
   220	| Review & Edit | Let user modify tool arguments | Edit API call parameters |
   221	| Feedback Loop | Iterative refinement with human feedback | Content generation review |
   222	| Follow-up | Agent asks for clarification | Missing information prompts |
   223	
   224	## Complete Clarification Example
   225	
   226	```go
   227	// Tool that asks user for clarification
   228	type askInput struct {
   229	    Question string `json:"question" jsonschema_description:"Question to ask the user"`
   230	}
   231	
   232	askTool, _ := utils.InferTool("ask_user", "Ask user for clarification",
   233	    func(ctx context.Context, input *askInput) (string, error) {
   234	        // Interrupt to ask user
   235	        return "", compose.Interrupt(ctx, input.Question)
   236	    })
   237	
   238	// ... configure agent with askTool ...
   239	
   240	// Handle interrupt
   241	iter := runner.Query(ctx, "Recommend me some books", adk.WithCheckPointID("session-1"))
   242	var interruptID string
   243	for {
   244	    event, ok := iter.Next()
   245	    if !ok {
   246	        break
   247	    }
   248	    if event.Action != nil && event.Action.Interrupted != nil {
   249	        interruptID = event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts[0].ID
   250	        fmt.Printf("Agent asks: %v\n", event.Action.Interrupted.InterruptContexts[0].Info)
   251	        break
   252	    }
   253	}
   254	
   255	// Resume with user's answer
   256	iter, _ = runner.ResumeWithParams(ctx, "session-1", &adk.ResumeParams{
   257	    Targets: map[string]any{
   258	        interruptID: "I want fiction books",
   259	    },
   260	})
   261	```
